cards.back:
```
conf t
router ospf <process-id>          ! process-id local to router, 1-65535
```

cards.front: Set OSPF Router ID (manual — preferred over auto):
cards.back:
```
router ospf 1
 router-id <a.b.c.d>
 do clear ip ospf process          ! required to apply new RID
```

cards.front: Two ways to enable OSPF on an interface — `network` command (under router ospf):
cards.back:
```
router ospf 1
 network <ip-address> <wildcard-mask> area <area-id>
```
Wildcard = inverse of subnet mask. `0.0.0.255` matches /24.

cards.front: Two ways to enable OSPF on an interface — interface command (modern, preferred):
Make interface passive (advertise prefix but no Hellos sent):
cards.back:
```
router ospf 1
 passive-interface g0/0
 ! OR
 passive-interface default
 no passive-interface g0/1         ! exception
```

cards.front:
Change OSPF priority (DR election) on interface: `ip ospf priority <0-255>`
Priority [...] = ineligible for DR/BDR.
Set auto-cost reference bandwidth (default 100 Mbps, set higher for gig+ links):
cards.back:
```
router ospf 1
 auto-cost reference-bandwidth <Mbps>   ! e.g. 10000 for 10G baseline
```

cards.front: Match OSPF hello/dead timers (must match between neighbors):
cards.back:
```
interface g0/0
 ip ospf hello-interval <s>
 ip ospf dead-interval <s>
```

cards.front: Inject default route into OSPF domain (from ASBR):
cards.back:
```
router ospf 1
 default-information originate [always]
```
`always` = inject even if router has no own default route.

```
show ip ospf neighbor             ! list adjacencies, state, RID
show ip ospf neighbor detail
```
State should be `FULL` for working adjacency (or `2WAY` between DROthers).

```
show ip ospf interface             ! all interfaces
show ip ospf interface g0/0        ! one interface — cost, hello/dead, DR/BDR, neighbors
show ip ospf interface brief
```

```
show ip ospf                       ! process ID, RID, area count
show ip protocols                  ! all routing protocols summary
show ip ospf database              ! LSDB contents
```

cards.front: See OSPF routes in routing table:
cards.back:
```
show ip route ospf
```
Codes: `O` = intra-area, `O IA` = inter-area, `O E1`/`O E2` = external, `O N1`/`O N2` = NSSA external.

cards.front: Debug OSPF adjacency issues (use briefly — heavy CPU):
cards.back:
```
debug ip ospf adj
debug ip ospf hello
undebug all
```

cards.front: OSPF adjacency fails — checklist of must-match parameters:
cards.back:
1. Hello/Dead timers
2. Area ID
3. Subnet (same /mask on link)
4. Authentication
5. Stub flag (E-bit)
6. MTU
